OMG you have described EXACTLY what happened to me...it was truly life-shattering and I never told a soul - I was so terrified our four kids would have their lifestyle collapse. I could have a letter - even my womens' magazine was opened by him, he never passed on messages. I could make toast the right colour (burnt), beds had to have ' hospital corners', he hated me going out- especially with female friends- even to cinema. I never had money and had to dress the kids from charity shops. Rages. I lived in a constant state of anxiety : It crushed my spirit and my soul. He's someone else's bad husband now - although I take comfort in the thought he might just have met his match. The damage to me feels eternal - I still have dreadful nightmares and flashbacks . A message to all you women out there who may be suffering similarly: NO man is better than a BAD man - be brave and get away. It will be hard but YOU are worth it and never let anyone tell you any different !

Richard Madeley has visited the world's toughest prison in El Salvador where 3,000 shaven-headed inmates are crammed behind floor-to-ceiling bars with nothing to do. The broadcaster spent time inside the Terrorism Confinement Center (Cecot), where suspected gang members sit in metal bunks stacked four beds high in concrete cells. The facility has become the cornerstone of El Salvador President Nayib Bukele's war on drug cartels and key to deportations from the US under President Donald Trump.

  • 1 video

Jeremy Zielinski, 75, and his wife Elaine, 80, have almost completed the process of dismantling the property, a year after a planning inspector backed their local council's decision. May Hayat, 33, from Tel Aviv, is one of several survivors who have arrived in London ahead of a new immersive exhibition about the atrocities at the Nova Festival in southern Israel, on October 7, 2023. Some 413 people were killed and 44 taken hostage to Gaza from the annual outdoor trance festival, as Hamas terrorists inflicted similar barbarities in nearby Kibbutzim, including Be'eri, Kfar Aza and Nir Oz. Visitors to the exhibition, which runs for six weeks in Shoreditch, can experience the full horror of Nova - where more than 3,000 music-loving young people had gathered together to dance through the night - as it unfolded.

Locals have been left furious after an annual horse fair dating back 750 years sparked parking chaos, with vehicles parked everywhere from a World War II memorial to a graveyard. Crowds of travellers descended on the idyllic Hampshire village of Wickham, which has a population of just 2,000 people, in a tradition that harks back generations. Horses and ponies lined the streets alongside their owners hoping to sell them, and attendees could be seen strolling through the square carrying long whips.
  • 3 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Fury as 100s of travellers descend on village for centuries-old fair

That’s so fake!

The pontiff, 70, has been known for his penchant for an American classic, with devoted Catholics even shipping his favourite pizza from Chicago on dry ice. But he recently told worshippers outside the Vatican that he likes 'mustard and ketchup' on his hotdog. One worshipper said to the first American pope, previously known as Cardinal Robert Prevost: 'Being from Chicago you've had more hot dogs than any hope in history.' The pontiff then amusingly replied 'only with mustard' before quickly correcting himself to say 'mustard and ketchup'. His comments have clearly been picked up by his followers, with one throwing a stuffed hotdog toy towards Pope Leo, who appears to try and catch it.
  • 56 comments
  • share Pope has hotdog thrown to him after admitting he likes the fast food
Got a story for Daily Mail?
Contact us at: tips@dailymail.com
Email

To be honest, I'd much prefer to be a traditional housewife. I'm nearly 50 and have been a full-time PA since leaving university, so 25 years of mundane, boring, tedious work. I've never enjoyed working and only do it because what option is there unless you marry a rich man? I enjoy cooking and keeping a clean, tidy house, and love welcoming my husband home from work every day, even if I've only just finished working myself. Unless you want a really exciting, inspiring, fulfilling career (which I never have) then what's wrong with 'keeping house'. The old fashioned husband/wife dynamics seem sensible to me and everyone's happy. Both people working leaves no free time to do nice things as you're having to do household work in the evenings and weekends.

Grace Olivier, from Doncaster, South Yorkshire, married her husband, Moses Olivier, 18 months ago and the couple have both assumed 'traditional' roles in their marriage.
  • 92 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Controversial tradwife reveals the key to her successful marriage

Adorable..I am delighted at this news..Stay safe and healthy.

A British zoo has welcomed its first ring-tailed lemur babies in 13 years - and keepers were stunned to discover the new arrivals are twins. The two male infants were born on April 12 to seven-year-old mum Eve, who only arrived at the Sussex zoo earlier this year as part of a new lemur troop.
  • 6 comments
  • share UK zoo welcomes not one but TWO ring-tailed lemur babies
